intro: Osteospermum caulescens, also known as the Cape daisy or bush daisy, is a perennial succulent plant native to the Western Cape province of South Africa. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Osteospermum caulescens include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
